questions.q: What is the title of the poem 'Childhood' by Rabindranath Tagore?
opts: (A) My Childhood
(B) The Childhood
(C) Reminiscences of Childhood
(D) Memories of Childhood
questions.explanation: The title of the poem is indeed 'My Childhood' as it is a personal reflection of the poet's childhood experiences.

questions.q: What is the central theme of the poem 'Childhood'?
opts: (A) The beauty of nature
(B) The joys of childhood
(C) The importance of innocence
(D) The inevitability of loss
questions.explanation: The central theme of the poem is the inevitability of loss, as the speaker reminisces about the passing of childhood and the loss of innocence.

questions.q: Who is the speaker of the poem 'Childhood'?
opts: (A) The poet himself
(B) A child
(C) A narrator
(D) The poet's friend
questions.explanation: The speaker of the poem is indeed the poet himself, as he reflects on his own childhood experiences.

questions.q: What is the tone of the poem 'Childhood'?
opts: (A) Melancholic
(B) Nostalgic
(C) Joyful
(D) Sarcastic
questions.explanation: The tone of the poem is nostalgic, as the speaker reminiscences about his childhood with a sense of longing and wistfulness.

questions.q: What is the significance of the line 'A little boy, with a flapping cap' in the poem?
opts: (A) It describes the poet's childhood self
(B) It highlights the importance of clothing in childhood
(C) It symbolizes the poet's growth into adulthood
(D) It is a metaphor for the passing of time
questions.explanation: The line 'A little boy, with a flapping cap' indeed describes the poet's childhood self, as he reflects on his own childhood experiences.

questions.q: What is the poet's attitude towards his childhood?
opts: (A) He is proud of his childhood
(B) He is nostalgic about his childhood
(C) He is ashamed of his childhood
(D) He is indifferent to his childhood
questions.explanation: The poet's attitude towards his childhood is one of nostalgia, as he reminiscences about the passing of childhood with a sense of longing and wistfulness.

questions.q: What is the role of nature in the poem 'Childhood'?
opts: (A) It is a source of joy and wonder
(B) It is a symbol of the passing of time
(C) It is a metaphor for the poet's childhood
(D) It is a distraction from the poet's memories
questions.explanation: Nature plays a significant role in the poem, serving as a source of joy and wonder for the poet as a child.

questions.q: What is the significance of the line 'The sky was once more pure' in the poem?
opts: (A) It describes the poet's childhood self
(B) It highlights the importance of the sky in childhood
(C) It symbolizes the passing of time
(D) It is a metaphor for the poet's growth into adulthood
questions.explanation: The line 'The sky was once more pure' indeed symbolizes the passing of time, as the poet reflects on the changes that have occurred in the world around him.

questions.q: What is the poet's view on the concept of 'childhood'?
opts: (A) It is a stage of innocence and wonder
(B) It is a stage of growth and development
(C) It is a stage of loss and nostalgia
(D) It is a stage of indifference
questions.explanation: The poet views childhood as a stage of innocence and wonder, as he reflects on the joys and simplicity of his own childhood experiences.

questions.q: What is the tone of the line 'The sky was once more pure' in the poem?
opts: (A) Melancholic
(B) Nostalgic
(C) Joyful
(D) Sarcastic
questions.explanation: The tone of the line 'The sky was once more pure' is indeed joyful, as the poet reflects on the beauty and wonder of the world around him.

questions.q: What is the poet's attitude towards the passing of time?
opts: (A) He is indifferent to the passing of time
(B) He is nostalgic about the passing of time
(C) He is ashamed of the passing of time
(D) He is bitter about the passing of time
questions.explanation: The poet's attitude towards the passing of time is one of nostalgia, as he reminiscences about the passing of childhood with a sense of longing and wistfulness.

questions.q: What is the significance of the line 'The world was once more full of wonder' in the poem?
opts: (A) It describes the poet's childhood self
(B) It highlights the importance of wonder in childhood
(C) It symbolizes the passing of time
(D) It is a metaphor for the poet's growth into adulthood
questions.explanation: The line 'The world was once more full of wonder' indeed highlights the importance of wonder in childhood, as the poet reflects on the joys and simplicity of his own childhood experiences.

questions.q: What is the poet's view on the concept of 'innocence'?
opts: (A) It is a state of ignorance and naivety
(B) It is a state of experience and knowledge
(C) It is a state of loss and nostalgia
(D) It is a state of indifference
questions.explanation: The poet views innocence as a state of experience and knowledge, as he reflects on the joys and simplicity of his own childhood experiences.

questions.q: What is the poet's attitude towards the world around him?
opts: (A) He is detached from the world
(B) He is nostalgic about the world
(C) He is ashamed of the world
(D) He is bitter about the world
questions.explanation: The poet's attitude towards the world around him is one of nostalgia, as he reminiscences about the passing of childhood with a sense of longing and wistfulness.

opts: (A) It describes the poet's childhood self
(B) It highlights the importance of beauty in childhood
(C) It symbolizes the passing of time
(D) It is a metaphor for the poet's growth into adulthood
questions.explanation: The line 'The world was once more full of beauty' indeed highlights the importance of beauty in childhood, as the poet reflects on the joys and simplicity of his own childhood experiences.
